content.text:
<user_query>
You are a senior Full Stack Developer, UI/UX Designer, and SEO Expert.

Build a complete production-ready website using Next.js 15, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and App Router.

Project Name:
Surat House Finish

Business:
Home Improvement Company in Surat, Gujarat.

Tagline:
Transforming Homes with Trusted Professionals

Goal:
Generate maximum leads through "Get Free Quote" and "WhatsApp Now" buttons.

Design Style:
Create a premium, clean, modern, luxury design inspired by Apple, Urban Company, Airbnb, and Stripe.

The website should be:
- Mobile First
- Fully Responsive
- Fast Loading
- SEO Optimized
- Accessible
- Premium UI
- Smooth but minimal animations
- Modern spacing
- Rounded cards
- Professional typography
- Conversion focused

Color Palette:
Primary: #0F172A
Secondary: #FBBF24
Background: #FFFFFF
Text: #1F2937

Typography:
Use modern Google Fonts like Inter.

Create proper reusable components.

Project Structure:

app/
components/
public/
styles/
lib/
hooks/
types/

Create reusable components.

Homepage Sections:

1. Sticky Navbar
- Logo
- Home
- Services
- About
- Gallery
- Reviews
- Contact
- Get Quote Button

2. Hero Section
Heading:
Surat's Trusted Home Improvement Experts

Subheading:
Painting, Interior Design, AC Repair, Plumbing, Electrical & Furniture Services — All Under One Roof.

Buttons:
- Get Free Quote
- WhatsApp Now

Hero image should represent premium home renovation.

3. Trust Badges
- Verified Professionals
- Affordable Pricing
- On-Time Service
- Quality Guaranteed
- Free Site Visit

4. Services Section

Create beautiful service cards for:

- House Painting
- Interior Design
- AC Service & Repair
- Plumbing
- Electrical Services
- Furniture & Carpentry

Each card should contain:
- Icon
- Title
- Description
- Learn More button

5. Why Choose Us

Professional layout with icons.

Include:
- Experienced Team
- Affordable Pricing
- Genuine Materials
- Fast Service
- Clean Work
- Customer Satisfaction

6. Process Section

Step 1
Book Free Visit

Step 2
Inspection

Step 3
Quotation

Step 4
Work Starts

Step 5
Project Delivery

7. Before & After Gallery

Responsive image gallery.

8. Customer Reviews

Beautiful testimonial cards.

9. FAQ Section

Accordion.

10. CTA Section

Large premium section.

Heading:
Ready to Transform Your Home?

Buttons:
Get Free Quote
WhatsApp Now

11. Footer

Include:
Company
Services
Quick Links
Contact
WhatsApp
Email
Location
Copyright

Services Pages:

Create separate pages for:
- Painting
- Interior Design
- Plumbing
- Electrical
- Furniture
- AC Repair

Each page should contain:
Hero
Benefits
Gallery
FAQ
CTA

SEO:

Generate metadata for every page.

Include:
Title
Description
Keywords
Open Graph
Twitter Cards

Performance:

Optimize images.
Lazy loading.
Reusable components.

Coding Standards:

- Clean architecture
- Reusable components
- TypeScript
- Tailwind
- Proper folder structure
- No duplicate code
- Production-ready code
- Follow Next.js best practices

Important:
Generate the website module by module.
Do not generate placeholder code.
Create professional production-ready components.
</user_query>

I'll build the Surat House Finish site module by module. First I'm checking the workspace and scaffolding the Next.js 15 project structure.

[REDACTED]

Scaffold Next.js project with full permissions

Create lib, types, hooks, and shared utilities
Build reusable UI components (Button, Section, Card, etc.)
Create service pages with SEO metadata

input.contents:
{
  "name": "surat-house-finish",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev --turbopack",
    "build": "next build",
    "start": "next start",
    "lint": "next lint"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "next": "^15.1.0",
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "lucide-react": "^0.468.0",
    "clsx": "^2.1.1",
    "tailwind-merge": "^2.6.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^22.10.0",
    "@types/react": "^19.0.0",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "typescript": "^5.7.0",
    "tailwindcss": "^3.4.16",
    "postcss": "^8.4.49",
    "autoprefixer": "^10.4.20",
    "eslint": "^9.16.0",
    "eslint-config-next": "^15.1.0"
  }
}

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2017",
    "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"],
    "allowJs": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "strict": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "module": "esnext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "jsx": "preserve",
    "incremental": true,
    "plugins": [{ "name": "next" }],
    "paths": {
      "@/*": ["./*"]
    }
  },
  "include": ["next-env.d.ts", "**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", ".next/types/**/*.ts"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules"]
}

# dependencies
/node_modules
/.pnp
.pnp.*
.yarn/*
!.yarn/patches
!.yarn/plugins
!.yarn/releases
!.yarn/versions

# testing
/coverage

# next.js
/.next/
/out/

# production
/build

# misc
.DS_Store
*.pem

# debug
npm-debug.log*
yarn-debug.log*
yarn-error.log*
.pnpm-debug.log*

# env files
.env*
!.env.example

# typescript
*.tsbuildinfo
next-env.d.ts

# vercel
.vercel

import {
  AirVent,
  Award,

  Users,

  Zap,
  type LucideIcon,
} from "lucide-react";

const iconMap: Record<string, LucideIcon> = {
  AirVent,
  Award,

  Zap,
};

export function getIcon(name: string): LucideIcon {
  return iconMap[name] ?? ShieldCheck;
}

I'd like a free quote.\n\nName: ${formData.name}\nPhone: ${formData.phone}\nService: ${formData.service || "General Inquiry"}\nMessage: ${formData.message}`; window.open(formatWhatsAppLink(message), "_blank"); }; return (
setFormData({ ...formData, name: e.target.value })} className="w-full rounded-xl border border-border bg-surface py-3 pl-11 pr-4 text-text outline-none transition-colors focus:border-secondary focus:ring-2 focus:ring-secondary/20" />
setFormData({ ...formData, phone: e.target.value })} className="w-full rounded-xl border border-border bg-surface py-3 pl-11 pr-4 text-text outline-none transition-colors focus:border-secondary focus:ring-2 focus:ring-secondary/20" />

# Surat House Finish

Premium home improvement website for **Surat House Finish** — a home improvement company in Surat, Gujarat.

## Tech Stack

- Next.js 15 (App Router)
- React 19
- TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS
- Lucide React Icons

## Getting Started

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000).

## Build

```bash
npm run build
npm start
```

## Project Structure

```
app/           # Next.js App Router pages & layouts
components/    # Reusable UI, layout, and section components
lib/           # Constants, SEO helpers, utilities
hooks/         # Custom React hooks
types/         # TypeScript type definitions
public/        # Static assets
styles/        # Global styles (app/globals.css)
```

## Configuration

Update business details in `lib/constants.ts`:

- Phone & WhatsApp number
- Email & address
- Site URL for SEO

## Services Pages

- `/services/painting`
- `/services/interior-design`
- `/services/ac-repair`
- `/services/plumbing`
- `/services/electrical`
- `/services/furniture`
/* Styles directory - global styles live in app/globals.css */

input.description: Compare workspace paths and link types
Start Next.js dev server for preview

content.text:
The **Surat House Finish** website is built and production-ready. One blocker: **`D:\surathf` is read-only** for your user account, so the project lives at:

**`C:\Users\abc\.cursor\projects\d-surathf`**

The production build completed successfully (12 static pages). Dev server is running at **http://localhost:3000**.

---

## Fix workspace permissions (to use `D:\surathf`)

Run PowerShell **as Administrator**:

```powershell
icacls "D:\surathf" /grant "$env:USERNAME:(OI)(CI)F"
```

Then copy the project:

```powershell
robocopy "C:\Users\abc\.cursor\projects\d-surathf" "D:\surathf" /E /XD node_modules .next
cd D:\surathf
npm install
npm run dev
```

Or open **`C:\Users\abc\.cursor\projects\d-surathf`** directly in Cursor.

---

## What was built

### Homepage (11 sections)
| Section | Features |
|---------|----------|
| **Sticky Navbar** | Logo, nav links, mobile menu, Get Quote CTA |
| **Hero** | Headline, stats, dual CTAs, premium image |
| **Trust Badges** | 5 conversion badges with icons |
| **Services** | 6 service cards with images + Learn More |
| **Why Choose Us** | 6 benefit cards |
| **Process** | 5-step workflow |
| **Gallery** | Interactive before/after viewer |
| **Reviews** | 6 testimonial cards with star ratings |
| **FAQ** | Animated accordion |
| **Contact Form** | Quote form → WhatsApp integration |
| **CTA + Footer** | Dual CTAs, full footer with contact info |

### Service pages (dynamic route)
- `/services/painting`
- `/services/interior-design`
- `/services/ac-repair`
- `/services/plumbing`
- `/services/electrical`
- `/services/furniture`

Each includes: Hero, Benefits, Gallery, FAQ, CTA — with full SEO metadata.

### Conversion features
- **Get Free Quote** buttons throughout
- **WhatsApp Now** buttons with pre-filled messages
- Floating WhatsApp button (bottom-right)
- Contact form sends structured quote requests via WhatsApp

### SEO & performance
- Per-page metadata (title, description, keywords, Open Graph, Twitter Cards)
- JSON-LD structured data (LocalBusiness schema)
- `sitemap.xml` and `robots.txt`
- Optimized images via `next/image` with lazy loading
- Static generation for all pages

---

## Project structure

```
app/                    → Pages, layout, globals.css, sitemap, robots
components/
  ui/                   → Button, Section, Accordion, ServiceCard, etc.
  layout/               → Navbar, Footer, WhatsAppFloat
  sections/             → Hero, Services, Gallery, FAQ, etc.
  services/             → ServicePageLayout
  seo/                  → JsonLd
lib/                    → constants, seo, utils, icons
hooks/                  → useScrollSpy, useScrolled
types/                  → TypeScript interfaces
styles/                 → Style reference
```

---

## Customize before launch

Update business details in `lib/constants.ts`:

- `phone` / `whatsapp` — your real numbers  
- `email` / `address`  
- `url` — your production domain  

---

## Commands

```bash
npm run dev      # Development (http://localhost:3000)
npm run build    # Production build
npm start        # Production server
```

---

Design uses your palette (**#0F172A**, **#FBBF24**, **#FFFFFF**, **#1F2937**), Inter typography, rounded cards, subtle animations, and a mobile-first layout inspired by Apple, Urban Company, Airbnb, and Stripe.

If you fix `D:\surathf` permissions, I can copy the project there and wire up anything else you need — custom logo, real photos, Google Analytics, or a contact API.

[REDACTED]
